svg.title: Fiscal appraisal distributions by denominator
svg.desc: Three violin panels compare fiscal appraisal per household, per square metre nationally, and per square metre in predominantly urban Neighborhood Units across the four IGVUST quartiles. The universes contain 6,849, 6,851, and 3,221 Neighborhood Units.

g.text: One geography, three readings of fiscal appraisal

text.style: font-size: 12.2px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #aeb9cc

g.text: The association changes with the denominator and the urban restriction; this is neither a social paradox nor a causal estimate.

text.style: font-size: 9.8px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #aeb9cc

g.text: Violins: Gaussian KDE, Scott bandwidth, cut=0, independent normalization. Inner lines: quartiles.

text.style: font-size: 9.8px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #aeb9cc

g.text: Source: SII + MDSF/IGVUST · Data cut 19 July 2026 · Positive finite appraisals only; no imputation.
